usage.title: Usage
usage.text:
To get started using this tool, you’ll need at least two files: an export of the department’s work order spreadsheet, and an export from the facilities services website. If you don’t know where to find these files, please ask your supervisor.

With these files downloaded to your computer, click the “upload” button on the homepage. Select the files one at a time from your file explorer. You should see them appear in the tray. Label the file you downloaded from the department's website as a “Department” upload by clicking the corresponding button. Label the file you downloaded from the facilities services website as a “Facilities” upload in the same manner.

Now click the file icon next to the clear button. This will prompt you to choose a path in your file explorer where you’d like the output of the tool to be saved. The name of this path should appear on screen when you’ve selected it.

Click the run button at the bottom of your screen to run the tool. If there were no errors, the output should be saved at the location you specified in a .csv file. This file can be uploaded back to the department spreadsheet to serve as the new record of work orders. If any changes were made to existing work orders, another file logging these changes will be saved to the output location as well.

debugging.title: Debugging
debugging.text:
If the app encounters an error, a message will popup explaining what happened. Not selecting an output path or trying to run the tool without files uploaded, for example, will produce errors. If there's a problem with the format of the files you upload, the popup will tell you what file and row the error occurred in.

The tool expects uploads to be of a specific format. If the data were malformed or the format of the department or facilities files were to change, you would get an error. In the best case, you can follow the error back to the file it occurred in and change cells to the proper format. Try setting cells to “N/A” if they are being particularly problematic. In the worst case, the format facilities services use for their exports might have changed completely. The app would no longer function.

In this case, feel free to contact me at the email provided below. If you’d like to attempt to fix it yourself or expand on the project as a whole, you can find a link to its github repository below.
